text:
The price of Brent crude, the global benchmark for oil, ended Friday afternoon at $103.14 a barrel. It was the highest settlement level in nearly four years. It reflected an increase of more than 40 percent since the start of war as fears spread in energy markets over shipping disruptions through a vital oil route in the Persian Gulf. Rising oil prices have not been stemmed significantly by an agreement by world leaders to release strategic oil reserves, nor by an announcement by the Trump administration to temporarily remove sanctions on Russian oil currently at sea.
The British government has warned U.K. citizens in the United Arab Emirates that they could be jailed or deported for “photographing, posting, or sharing images” of sites where missiles and drones have struck, as well as government buildings and diplomatic missions.
title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
